# String Manipulation in Python - Notes (2026-06-16)

## f-strings (Python 3.6+)

The modern and preferred way to format strings:

```python
name = "World"
greeting = f"Hello, {name}!"
# supports expressions
result = f"3 + 4 = {3 + 4}"
# format specifiers
pi = 3.14159
formatted = f"Pi ≈ {pi:.2f}"  # "Pi ≈ 3.14"
```

## Common String Methods

| Method                   | Description                                 |
| ------------------------ | ------------------------------------------- |
| `str.strip()`            | Remove leading/trailing whitespace          |
| `str.split(sep)`         | Split into list by separator                |
| `str.join(iterable)`     | Join iterable with this string as separator |
| `str.replace(old, new)`  | Replace all occurrences                     |
| `str.startswith(prefix)` | Check prefix                                |
| `str.endswith(suffix)`   | Check suffix                                |
| `str.lower() / upper()`  | Case conversion                             |
| `str.find(sub)`          | Find index of substring (-1 if not found)   |

## Strings are Immutable

All string methods return new strings. The original is never modified:

```python
s = "hello"
s.upper()       # returns "HELLO", s is still "hello"
s = s.upper()   # reassign variable to keep the new value
```
